highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 2.88.4 adds .NET 8 and Apple Silicon macOS support and refreshes bundled native libraries.
highlights_body: This release adds native ARM64 macOS binaries, .NET 8 WASM assets for Blazor, and moves the WinUI target onto `JSImport` on `net7.0`. It refreshes libpng, libexpat and zlib, ships the Android `.aar` inside the NuGet, and picks up Uno WASM improvements including EH+SIMD.

bullets.detail: Native binaries are now produced for ARM64 macOS machines, so `.NET on macOS-arm64` no longer runs SkiaSharp under Rosetta.
The new WebAssembly assets shipped with .NET 8 are now included so Blazor apps on .NET 8 pick up the right native.
New .NET iOS and Mac Catalyst samples plus a .NET "Core" sample host demonstrate the modern-platform bindings.
The Android `.aar` is now included in the NuGet package so consumers no longer have to source it separately.
All build-time requirements on the .NET 5 SDK have been removed.

bullets.lead: WinUI moves to JSImport on net7.0
bullets.detail: The WinUI target now uses `JSImport` on `net7.0`, aligning with the modern WinUI/Uno interop model.
`SKColor` hex-string parsing was rewritten to avoid the per-character allocations and lookups it used to do.
`SKCanvasView` no longer throws when the view is disposed before it has been rendered for the first time.
`SKXamlCanvas` on WASM tolerates a missing canvas element, updates the bitmap `SKAlphaType` correctly, and disambiguates `SKGLView` between Uno and MAUI targets.

bullets.lead: Uno Opaque defaults on Apple platforms
bullets.detail: `SKXamlCanvas` on Uno now defaults `Opaque` to `false` on macOS and iOS to match the platform expectation, and `SKSwapChainPanel` was updated for Android and iOS.
The Tizen views now compute canvas size correctly on high-DPI displays.
The `ToRect` extension no longer returns wrong bounds in an edge case.

bullets.lead: Guard against a race in the view lifecycle
bullets.detail: An additional check avoids an exception in a lifecycle race path.
libpng bumped to 1.6.40, libexpat to 2.5.0, and zlib to 1.2.13 — pulling in the latest upstream security and correctness fixes.
Several internal blocks were rewritten with C# 8 block-scoped `using` statements to reduce nesting.

contributor_summaries.jeromelaban: Moved WinUI to `JSImport` on net7.0, disambiguated `SKGLView`, and enabled WASM EH+SIMD in all configurations for net8+
contributor_summaries.roubachof: Fixed the Uno `SKXamlCanvas` Alpha type on WASM, defaulted `Opaque` on Apple platforms, and updated the Android and iOS `SKSwapChainPanel`
contributor_summaries.pjcollins: Contributed to the modern-platform enablement work landing in this release
contributor_summaries.dellis1972: Made the Android `.aar` ship inside the NuGet package
contributor_summaries.FoggyFinder: Added a defensive check to avoid an exception in a lifecycle race path
contributor_summaries.jjzhang12: Fixed the `SKCanvasView` dispose exception when the view is disposed before it has been rendered
contributor_summaries.jwikberg: Rewrote hex-string parsing in `SKColor` for a large performance win
contributor_summaries.Lehonti: Removed nesting by adopting block-scoped `using` statements
contributor_summaries.myroot: Fixed Tizen canvas-size calculation on high-DPI displays
contributor_summaries.Redth: Contributed to the modern-platform enablement work landing in this release

preview_summaries.v2.88.4-preview.96: Preview 96 shipped the final Hot Restart native reference.
preview_summaries.v2.88.4-preview.95: Preview 95 landed the libpng/libexpat refresh, WinUI JSImport move, the SKCanvasView disambiguation and the `using`-scope cleanup.
preview_summaries.v2.88.4-preview.82: Preview 82 added the .NET 8 WASM assets and the ToRect fix.
preview_summaries.v2.88.4-preview.76: Preview 76 added the .NET iOS/Catalyst samples, faster SKColor hex parsing and ARM64 macOS binaries.
preview_summaries.v2.88.4-preview.70: Preview 70 opened the line with zlib 1.2.13, Uno Opaque and swap-chain fixes, and the drop of the .NET 5 SDK requirement.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 2.8.2.4 adds native binaries for Apple Silicon macOS, ships the Hot Restart native reference, and enables WASM multi-threading and SIMD alongside SkiaSharp.
